# LLM.TXT
# Project: Seed Code CLI
# Author: Al Shahriar Sowan
# License: MIT

## Project Overview

Seed Code is a modern open-source AI CLI designed to provide a fast, clean and developer-friendly terminal experience.

The goal is to make interacting with multiple AI providers feel as simple as using a native shell command.

Example:

seedcode
> Explain Python decorators
> Generate React component
> Fix this code
> Continue previous chat

The CLI should feel lightweight, responsive and beautiful.

---

## Core Principles

- Terminal-first experience
- Fast startup
- Streaming responses
- Minimal dependencies
- Cross-platform
- Easy to extend
- Clean architecture
- Simple configuration
- Open source

---

## Supported Platforms

- Windows
- Linux
- macOS

---

## Supported Providers

The architecture should support multiple providers.

Examples:

- OpenRouter
- AeroLink
- Anthropic-compatible APIs
- OpenAI-compatible APIs
- Local OpenAI-compatible servers

Providers must be modular.

Never hardcode provider logic.

---

## Architecture

High level layers:

CLI

↓

Application

↓

Conversation Manager

↓

Provider Client

↓

HTTP Transport

↓

AI Model

Each layer should have one responsibility.

---

## Coding Rules

Prefer

- Python 3.12+
- Type hints
- Dataclasses where appropriate
- Async I/O
- Rich for terminal UI
- httpx for networking
- pathlib instead of os.path
- logging module
- small reusable functions

Avoid

- Global mutable state
- Huge files
- Circular imports
- Deep inheritance
- Blocking network requests

---

## Style

Write code that is

- readable
- maintainable
- modular
- documented

Names should be descriptive.

Avoid abbreviations.

---

## Error Handling

Never silently ignore errors.

Provide actionable messages.

Example

✓ Connected

✗ Invalid API key

✗ Provider unavailable

✗ Network timeout

Always fail gracefully.

---

## Configuration

Configuration should be stored separately from source code.

Support

API key

Default provider

Default model

Theme

History

Custom endpoints

Future settings should be backward compatible.

---

## Streaming

Streaming is a first-class feature.

Responses should render token-by-token whenever supported.

Fallback to normal responses if streaming is unavailable.

---

## Commands

Potential commands

seedcode

seedcode chat

seedcode login

seedcode models

seedcode providers

seedcode update

seedcode doctor

seedcode config

seedcode history

seedcode clear

seedcode version

---

## Extensibility

Adding a provider should require minimal changes.

Prefer plugin-like architecture.

New providers should implement a common interface.

---

## Security

Never

- log API keys
- expose secrets
- commit credentials
- print tokens

Mask sensitive values.

Validate configuration.

---

## Documentation

Every public module should contain

- purpose
- usage
- examples

Complex logic should include comments explaining WHY rather than WHAT.

---

## Testing

Prefer

pytest

Test

- parsing
- provider selection
- configuration
- streaming
- retries
- failures

---

## Performance

Optimize for

Fast startup

Low memory usage

Responsive streaming

Minimal network overhead

---

## User Experience

The CLI should feel premium.

Requirements

- Beautiful terminal output
- Clean spacing
- Helpful errors
- Sensible defaults
- Fast responses

Avoid unnecessary prompts.

---

## Design Philosophy

Seed Code should feel like

"the VS Code of AI command-line tools"

Professional.

Minimal.

Reliable.

Powerful.

---

## Future Goals

- Plugin system
- MCP integration
- Local models
- Tool calling
- Agent mode
- File editing
- Image generation
- Voice support
- Shell execution
- Workspace awareness

---

## AI Agent Instructions

If you are modifying this project:

- Preserve architecture.
- Preserve modularity.
- Do not introduce breaking changes without reason.
- Prefer extending existing abstractions.
- Keep files small.
- Keep commits focused.
- Follow existing naming conventions.
- Maintain backward compatibility whenever possible.
- Update documentation when behavior changes.
- Add tests for new functionality.

When uncertain, choose the simplest maintainable solution.

End of file.
